title: Protocol Amending the Agreement of the Establishment of a Fund for ASEAN
text:
Protocol to Amend the Agreement for the Establishment of a
Fund for ASEAN
Bangkok, 22 October 1982
WHEREAS the Governments of the Republic of Indonesia, Malaysia, the Republic of the
Philippines, the Republic of Singapore and the Kingdom of Thailand, being members of the
Association of Southeast Asian Nations (hereinafter referred to as “ASEAN”), have on the 17th
December, 1969 signed an Agreement for the establishment of a fund for ASEAN.
AND WHEREAS the contracting parties desire to make a certain amendment to the rules set out
in the Annex to the Agreement.
AND WHEREAS under Article IV of the Agreement it is provided that amendments may be
made to the Agreement and the Rules subject to the acceptance of all the contracting parties.
NOW BY THIS PROTOCOL the parties have agreed as follows:
1. The figure “US$ 2,500″ appearing in rule 14 (f) of the Rules set out in the Annex to the
Agreement shall be amended and be substituted by the figure “US$ 10,000″.
2. This Protocol shall enter into force on the date of signature of all the contracting parties.
IN WITNESS WHEREOF the undersigned, being duly authorised thereto by their
respective governments, have signed this Protocol.
DONE at Bangkok, in a single copy in the English Language this 22nd day of October
1982.
For the Government of the Republic of Indonesia:
(sgd)
AIR MARSHAL SOEBAMBANG
Ambassador of the Republic of Indonesia
For the Government of Malaysia:
(sgd)
DATUK SHAHUDDIN BIN MOHAMAD TAIB

Ambassador of Malaysia
For the Government of the
Republic of the Philippines :
(sgd)
LT. GENERAL RAFAEL M. ILETO
Ambassador of the Republic of the Philippines
For the Government of the
Republic of Singapore:
(sgd)
TAN KENG JIN
Director General, ASEAN – Singapore
For the Government of the
Kingdom of Thailand:
(sgd)
ACM SIDDHI SAVETSILA
Minister of Foreign Affairs
